prerequisites: A HubSpot account where the app will be installed. The user performing the installation must be a Super Admin or have HubSpot Marketplace Access permissions.
A HubSpot app from which you obtain the OAuth client ID and client secret (configured under the credentials field).
OAuth scopes selected for the app that cover the HubSpot API resources you intend to write to. Scope availability depends on the account subscription tier.
Optionally, an object storage bucket and its credentials if you populate the object_storage_config field for staging.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code grant)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with HubSpot using OAuth, supplied through the credentials field as client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token. You create a HubSpot app to get the client ID and client secret, send the user through HubSpot's OAuth authorization flow, then exchange the returned authorization code at https://api.hubapi.com/oauth/v3/token for an access token and a refresh token. The refresh token is used to mint new access tokens.

authMethods.name: Object storage access keys (optional)
authMethods.summary: If the optional object_storage_config field is set for staging, provide the storage credentials via access_key_id and secret_access_key. These are separate from HubSpot OAuth and authenticate to the configured object storage backend rather than the HubSpot API.

setupSteps.title: Create a HubSpot app
setupSteps.body: In your HubSpot account, create an app. Its settings page provides the OAuth client ID and client secret. Configure a redirect URI and select the scopes your app needs. Scopes determine which HubSpot API endpoints and data the app can access.

setupSteps.title: Complete the OAuth authorization flow
setupSteps.body: Build a HubSpot authorization URL that includes your client ID, redirect URI, and URL-encoded scopes, and have a user with Super Admin or Marketplace Access permissions grant access. HubSpot returns an authorization code to your redirect URI.

setupSteps.title: Exchange the code for a refresh token
setupSteps.body: POST the authorization code (along with the client ID and client secret) to https://api.hubapi.com/oauth/v3/token to receive an access token and a refresh token. Use the refresh token to obtain new access tokens as needed.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Populate the credentials field with credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token from the steps above.

setupSteps.title: Configure object storage (optional)
setupSteps.body: If you use staging via object_storage_config, supply object_storage_config.access_key_id and object_storage_config.secret_access_key for the storage backend. Leave object_storage_config unset if not required.
